# IPNetwork

> IPNetwork is a .NET library and CLI tool for IPv4/IPv6 network calculations: subnetting, supernetting, CIDR, netmask, containment, overlap, subtraction, and IP enumeration.

## Installation

### CLI (Native AOT, no .NET runtime required)

macOS / Linux:
```
brew install lduchosal/ipnetwork/ipnetwork
```

Windows:
```
choco install ipnetwork
```

### .NET Library

```
dotnet add package IPNetwork2
```

## CLI Usage

```
ipnetwork [-inmcbflu] [-j] [-d cidr|-D] [-h|-s cidr|-S network|-w|-W|-x|-C network|-o network] networks ...
```

### Print options (select which fields to display)

- `-i` : network (CIDR notation)
- `-n` : network address
- `-m` : netmask
- `-c` : CIDR prefix length
- `-b` : broadcast address
- `-f` : first usable IP address
- `-l` : last usable IP address
- `-u` : number of usable IP addresses
- `-t` : total number of IP addresses

When no print option is specified, all fields are displayed.

### Output options

- `-j` : JSON output (default is human-readable text)

### Parse options

- `-d cidr` : use this CIDR if not provided in input (default /32)
- `-D` : IPv4 only - use default CIDR based on class (A/8, B/16, C/24)

### Actions

- `-h` : display help
- `-s cidr` : split network into subnets of given CIDR
  - Example: `ipnetwork -s 24 10.0.0.0/8`
- `-w` : supernet networks into smallest possible subnets
  - Example: `ipnetwork -w 10.0.0.0/24 10.0.1.0/24`
- `-W` : supernet networks into one single subnet
  - Example: `ipnetwork -W 10.0.0.0/24 10.0.10.0/24`
- `-x` : list all IP addresses in networks
  - Example: `ipnetwork -x 10.0.0.0/30`
- `-C network` : test if network contains other networks
  - Example: `ipnetwork -C 10.0.0.0/8 10.0.1.0/24`
- `-o network` : test if network overlaps with other networks
  - Example: `ipnetwork -o 10.0.0.0/8 192.168.0.0/16`
- `-S network` : subtract network from other networks
  - Example: `ipnetwork -S 10.0.1.0/24 10.0.0.0/23`

### Input formats

Networks can be specified as:
- `10.0.0.0/8` (CIDR notation)
- `10.0.0.0/255.0.0.0` (netmask notation)
- `1.2.3.4` (single IP, defaults to /32)
- `2001:db8::/32` (IPv6 CIDR)
- `2001:db8:1:2:3:4:5:6/128` (IPv6 host)

## CLI Examples

### Display network information

```
$ ipnetwork 10.0.0.0/8

IPNetwork   : 10.0.0.0/8
Network     : 10.0.0.0
Netmask     : 255.0.0.0
Cidr        : 8
Broadcast   : 10.255.255.255
FirstUsable : 10.0.0.1
LastUsable  : 10.255.255.254
Usable      : 16777214
Total       : 16777216
```

### JSON output

```
$ ipnetwork -j 192.168.1.0/24

[
  {
    "ipnetwork": "192.168.1.0/24",
    "network": "192.168.1.0",
    "netmask": "255.255.255.0",
    "cidr": 24,
    "broadcast": "192.168.1.255",
    "firstUsable": "192.168.1.1",
    "lastUsable": "192.168.1.254",
    "usable": "254",
    "total": "256"
  }
]
```

### Select specific fields

```
$ ipnetwork -n -c 10.0.0.0/8

Network     : 10.0.0.0
Cidr        : 8
```

### Subnet

```
$ ipnetwork -s 9 10.0.0.0/8

IPNetwork   : 10.0.0.0/9
...
--
IPNetwork   : 10.128.0.0/9
...
```

### Supernet

```
$ ipnetwork -w 10.0.0.0/24 10.0.1.0/24

IPNetwork   : 10.0.0.0/23
...
```

### Containment test

```
$ ipnetwork -C 10.0.0.0/8 10.0.1.0/24

10.0.0.0/8 contains 10.0.1.0/24 : True
```

### Overlap test

```
$ ipnetwork -o 10.0.0.0/8 192.168.0.0/16

10.0.0.0/8 overlaps 192.168.0.0/16 : False
```

### Subtract

```
$ ipnetwork -S 10.0.1.0/24 10.0.0.0/23

10.0.0.0/24
```

### List IP addresses

```
$ ipnetwork -x 10.0.0.0/30

10.0.0.0
10.0.0.1
10.0.0.2
10.0.0.3
```

## C# Library API

Core class: `System.Net.IPNetwork2`

### Parse

```csharp
IPNetwork2 ipnetwork = IPNetwork2.Parse("192.168.168.100/24");
// Properties: .Network, .Netmask, .Broadcast, .Cidr,
//             .FirstUsable, .LastUsable, .Usable, .Total
```

### Contains / Overlap

```csharp
bool contains = ipnetwork.Contains(IPAddress.Parse("192.168.168.1")); // true
bool overlap = ipnetwork.Overlap(IPNetwork2.Parse("192.168.1.0/24")); // false
```

### Subnet

```csharp
IPNetworkCollection subnets = ipnetwork.Subnet(25);
// subnets[0] = 192.168.168.0/25
// subnets[1] = 192.168.168.128/25
```

### Supernet

```csharp
IPNetwork2[] supernet = IPNetwork2.Supernet(new[] {
    IPNetwork2.Parse("192.168.0.0/24"),
    IPNetwork2.Parse("192.168.1.0/24")
});
// supernet[0] = 192.168.0.0/23
```

### Subtract

```csharp
IEnumerable<IPNetwork2> result = network1.Subtract(network2);
```

### ParseRange

```csharp
IEnumerable<IPNetwork2> blocks = IPNetwork2.ParseRange("192.168.1.45 - 192.168.1.65");
// Converts IP range to minimal set of CIDR blocks
```

### IANA Reserved Blocks

```csharp
IPNetwork2.IANA_ABLK_RESERVED1  // 10.0.0.0/8
IPNetwork2.IANA_BBLK_RESERVED1  // 172.16.0.0/12
IPNetwork2.IANA_CBLK_RESERVED1  // 192.168.0.0/16
```

## Links

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/lduchosal/ipnetwork)
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ipnetwork2)
- [Homebrew Tap](https://github.com/lduchosal/homebrew-ipnetwork)
- [Chocolatey Package](https://community.chocolatey.org/packages/ipnetwork)
